headlines.title: Salesforce Downgraded On Views 'Agentforce, As A Product, Just Isn't There'
headlines.summary: KeyBanc downgraded Salesforce on views its AI strategy isn't jelling. Salesforce stock stumbled on the news as other software companies also retreated.

headlines.title: Workday (WDAY) Stock Looks Fairly Valued Despite Fresh AI Growth Optimism
headlines.summary: Workday stock has had a tough run over the past year, yet current valuation checks suggest it is not an obvious bargain even after the pullback. With recent headlines highlighting growth in its enterprise software business and renewed attention on its AI capabilities, investors are weighing whether the current price already reflects those strengths. Workday shares are down 42.

headlines.title: Workday (WDAY) Could Be 19% Undervalued After Risk Off Selling Hits Software
headlines.summary: Recent weakness in Workday (WDAY) has been tied to broader risk-off moves in growth software stocks after President Trump ended the Iran ceasefire, with higher oil prices and bond yields weighing on valuations. See our latest analysis for Workday. Beyond the latest sell off, Workday’s share price has been volatile, with a 21.95% 90 day share price return sitting against a year to date share price decline of 33% and a 1 year total shareholder return decline of 42.59%.

headlines.title: Workday (WDAY) Appoints John Lombard To Lead Asia Pacific Growth
headlines.summary: Workday (NasdaqGS:WDAY) has appointed John Lombard as President for Asia Pacific. Lombard will lead the company’s regional growth and go to market strategy across key APAC markets. The move highlights Workday’s focus on expanding its enterprise cloud applications footprint in Asia Pacific. For investors tracking Workday, the appointment comes at a time when the stock trades around $137.88, with the share price down 33.0% year to date and 42.6% over the past year.

headlines.title: Lovable reportedly in talks to double its valuation to $13.2B
headlines.summary: The $300 million round is expected to be led by Menlo Ventures, Sifted reported.

description: Workday, Inc. provides enterprise cloud applications in the United States and internationally. The company offers a suite of financial management applications to maintain accounting information; manage financial processes, such as payables and receivables; identify real-time financial, operational, and management insights; perform financial consolidation; reduce time-to-close; promote internal control and auditability; and achieve consistency across finance operations. It also provides spend management solutions that help organizations to streamline supplier selection and contract management, build and execute sourcing events, such as requests for proposals, and manage indirect spend; expense management solutions to submit and approve expenses; and a suite of human capital management applications that enables HR teams to hire, onboard, pay, develop, reskill, and provide employee experiences. In addition, the company offers planning applications. Further, it provides supply chain and inventory solutions to healthcare organizations; solutions to manage the end-to-end student and faculty lifecycle; Workday Extend for customers and their developers to build custom applications. The company serves the professional and business services, financial services, healthcare, manufacturing, media, education, government, technology, media, retail, and hospitality industries. It sells its solutions through its direct sales organization. The company was formerly known as North Tahoe Power Tools, Inc. and changed its name to Workday, Inc. in July 2005. Workday, Inc. was incorporated in 2005 and is headquartered in Pleasanton, California.
